description: Scoring rubric for all outbound PantryMate emails. Each criterion scored 0–10. Total possible: 70.

auto_approve.condition: total_score >= 60 AND no single score < 5 AND honesty_score >= 7

flag_for_review.action: Return to Godfather with specific notes. Revise and re-review.

auto_reject.action: Escalate to Wolfgang immediately. Do not revise without human approval.

criteria.label: Personalization
criteria.description: Email references the recipient's specific business name, business type, or something unique to their operation. Not a generic blast.

scoring_guide.10: Mentions business name AND business type AND something specific (city, owner name, or observed detail)
scoring_guide.8-9: Mentions business name AND business type
scoring_guide.4-5: Generic but has one token personalization (e.g., just first name)
scoring_guide.0-1: Completely generic, could be sent to anyone

automated_checks: Check for presence of {{business_name}} or actual resolved business name
Check for presence of business type keyword (restaurant, gym, bakery, etc.)
Flag if body contains unresolved template variables like {{, }}, [NAME], [BUSINESS]

criteria.label: Honesty
criteria.description: No false claims. No invented testimonials. No fabricated social proof. No guaranteed income claims.

scoring_guide.10: Every claim is verifiable or clearly framed as illustrative. No guarantees.
scoring_guide.8-9: Minor hedging issues (e.g., 'most clients' without data), but nothing deceptive
scoring_guide.7: Borderline — passes threshold but needs improvement
scoring_guide.5-6: Contains vague claims that could mislead ('many businesses doubled revenue')
scoring_guide.3-4: Contains unverifiable testimonials or strong implied guarantees
scoring_guide.0-2: Contains false claims, fake testimonials, or explicit income guarantees

automated_checks: Flag guaranteed income phrases: 'you will make', 'guaranteed to earn', 'double your revenue', 'triple your sales'
Flag fake urgency: 'only N spots left', 'expires tonight', 'last chance'
Flag fabricated social proof: 'our 10,000 happy clients', 'as seen on' without verifiable source
Flag superlatives used as facts: '#1 platform', 'best in the industry'
criteria.rejection_note: Honesty score below 7 triggers AUTO-REJECT regardless of total score. Escalate to Wolfgang.

criteria.label: Brevity
criteria.description: Email body (excluding subject line and signature) is under 120 words. Shorter is better for cold outreach.
automated_checks: Count words in body (exclude subject line, signature block)
Auto-score based on word count thresholds above
Flag if body contains multiple paragraphs without clear structure

criteria.label: Subject Line Relevance
criteria.description: Subject line accurately previews the email content. Not misleading, not clickbait, not disconnected from body.

scoring_guide.10: Subject clearly summarizes the email purpose, sounds natural, personalized if appropriate
scoring_guide.8-9: Relevant and honest, minor polish needed
scoring_guide.6-7: Loosely related but could be more specific
scoring_guide.4-5: Subject line is generic or only vaguely related to body
scoring_guide.2-3: Misleading or disconnected from body content

automated_checks: Check for 'Re:' or 'Fwd:' in subject of unsolicited emails (deceptive)
Check for ALL CAPS words in subject
Check for excessive punctuation: !!!, ???
Flag subject lines over 60 characters (may get clipped)

criteria.label: CTA Clarity
criteria.description: There is exactly one clear call to action. The recipient knows exactly what to do next. No multiple asks, no vague endings.

scoring_guide.10: One explicit CTA, clear next step, easy to take action (link, reply, book a call)
scoring_guide.8-9: One CTA, slightly vague wording but still actionable
scoring_guide.6-7: CTA present but buried or wordy
scoring_guide.4-5: Two competing CTAs or very vague ('reach out sometime')
scoring_guide.2-3: No clear CTA or just 'let me know what you think'

automated_checks: Count question marks — more than one may indicate multiple asks
Check for presence of action words: 'reply', 'click', 'book', 'schedule', 'visit'
Flag emails ending without any actionable request

criteria.label: Spam Risk
criteria.description: Email does not contain spam trigger words, excessive punctuation, ALL CAPS sections, or other patterns that cause deliverability issues.

scoring_guide.10: Clean — no trigger words, no ALL CAPS, no excessive punctuation
scoring_guide.8-9: One minor flag (e.g., word 'free' used naturally in context)
scoring_guide.6-7: Two minor flags or one moderate flag
scoring_guide.4-5: Multiple trigger words or one severe trigger word
scoring_guide.0-1: Reads like a spam email — would never reach inbox

automated_checks: Scan for spam trigger words: FREE, GUARANTEED, WINNER, URGENT, LIMITED TIME, ACT NOW, CLICK HERE, MAKE MONEY, EARN $, NO RISK, 100%
Check for ALL CAPS words (3+ consecutive caps words = flag)
Check for excessive punctuation: !!!, ???, !?!
Check for emoji overuse (more than 2 in a cold email)
Check for dollar signs in subject line

criteria.label: Professional Tone
criteria.description: Email sounds like it was written by a thoughtful person, not a sales robot. Not pushy, not needy, not overly formal. Confident and human.

scoring_guide.10: Warm, confident, conversational. Respects the reader's time and intelligence.
scoring_guide.8-9: Professional and clear, minor tone issues
scoring_guide.6-7: Adequate but slightly stiff or slightly pushy
scoring_guide.4-5: Clearly robotic, overly salesy, or condescending
scoring_guide.2-3: Pushy, aggressive, or dismissive of the reader

automated_checks: Flag excessive apologies: 'I know you're busy', 'I'm sorry to bother you', 'I won't take much of your time'
Flag aggressive phrases: 'You NEED this', 'Don't miss out', 'Your competitors are already using'
Flag needy closings: 'Please, please reply', 'I really hope to hear from you'
Flag overly formal openers: 'Dear Sir/Madam', 'To Whom It May Concern'

output_format.reviewer_notes: Hawk's summary verdict and key concerns
